MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-05-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

DISCOVERY 301, PLAINTIFF AMANDA SILK'S MOTION TO COMPEL VERIFIED RESPONSES AND FURTHER PRODUCTION TO HER SET ONE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. NO LATER THAN MARCH 18, 2026 DEFENDANT HELEN NAZZAL MUST SERVE A VERIFICATION FOR ITS RESPONSES TO THE SET ONE REQUESTS. A COPY OF THE RESERVATION OF RIGHTS IS ALSO DUE BY MARCH 18, 2026. THE COURT CREDITS NAZZAL'S UNTIMELY OPPOSITION THAT ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS IN HER POSSESSION, CUSTODY AND CONTROL HAVE BEEN PRODUCED. THAT OPPOSITION, HOWEVER, DOES NOT ADDRESS THE PORTION OF THE MOTION SEEKING A VERIFICATION. SILK'S SOLE GROUND FOR "MODEST" MONETARY SANCTIONS IS THAT SHE "WAS FORCED TO EXPEND TIME AND EFFORT PREPARING THIS MOTION." THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT HAS HELD THAT THIS IS NOT A PERMISSIBLE BASIS FOR DISCOVERY MONETARY SANCTIONS. (MUSAELIAN V. ADAMS (2009) 45 CAL. 4TH 512). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING IN COPMLAINE WITH CRC 3.1312. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/HEK)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT MODIFIES ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. CAUSE OF ACTION 3 IMPROPERLY COMBINES TWO CAUSES OF ACTION. CAUSE OF ACTION 4 IS UNCERTAIN AS TO WHEN AND TO WHOM PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINTS WERE COMMUNICATED, AND FAILS TO ALLEGE OTHER ELEMENTS OF SECTION 1942.5(A). CAUSES OF ACTION 5 AND 8 ARE UNCERTAIN FOR THE SAME REASON AS CAUSE OF ACTION 4. CAUSE OF ACTION 5 ALSO FAILS TO ELABORATE AS TO HOW "MISCLASSIFYING THE UNIT" WOULD VIOLATE THE RENT ORDINANCE. CAUSE OF ACTION 8 DOES NOT ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE THE ELEMENT OF IIED THAT THE CONDUCT WAS EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS. CAUSE OF ACTION 9 FAILS TO ALLEGE ELEMENTS OF AN INTERFERENCE CLAIM AND CONTRADICTS ITSELF AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S BUSINESS WAS EVER "OPERATED" BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT ALSO SAYS THE BUSINESS WAS "UNABLE TO LAUNCH." CAUSE OF ACTION 10 DOES NOT ALLEGE ELEMENTS OF A UCL VIOLATION. CAUSE OF ACTION 11 FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE IT DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT EXCEEDED THIS COURT'S ORDER. ON AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES, THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Verified Responses And Further Production To Request For Production, Set One; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-05-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
AMANDA SILK, ESQ., 559-916-0736 silkprofessionalservices@gmail.com Pro Per Plaintiff

Michael Vroman, Esq., PHILLIPS, SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP (415) 278-9400 mvroman@psalaw.net
Attorneys for Defendant HELEN NAZZAL

Hearing held. discussed, not ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as modified:

PLAINTIFF AMANDA SILK's, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA SILK AND LACE OCCASIONS, Motion To Compel Verified Responses And Further Production To Request For Production, Set One; Request For Sanctions.

Plaintiff Amanda Silk's motion to compel verified responses and further production to her set one requests for production and for monetary sanctions is granted in part.

No later than March 18, 2026 defendant Helen Nazzal must serve a verification for its responses to the set one requests. A copy of the Reservation of Rights is also due by March 18, 2026. The court credits Nazzal's untimely opposition that all responsive documents in her possession, custody and control have been produced. That opposition, however, does not address the portion of the motion seeking a verification. Silk's sole ground for "modest" monetary sanctions is that she "was forced to expend time and effort preparing this motion." The California Supreme Court has held that this is not a permissible basis for discovery monetary sanctions. (Musaelian v. Adams (2009) 45 Cal. 4th 512).

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in copmlaine with CRC 3.1312.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances: Michael Vroman of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP (415.278.9400) for defendant and moving party; Amanda Silk (559.916.0736) plaintiff and opposing party appearing in propria persona.

Discussed and the court modifies its ruling as follows: GRANTED, in part, and DENIED, in part.

The Court GRANTS the following requests to strike. Prayer No. 3 is stricken; the FAC does not allege sufficiently particular facts as to Defendant's mental state to support punitive damages. On agreement of the parties, the court grants plaintiff's request for leave to amend punitive damages. Paragraph 5 is stricken, with leave to amend, as an improper legal conclusion; San Francisco Planning Code Section 209.2 makes clear that only certain commercial uses, which are not presently alleged in the FAC, are permitted in RM-1 designations.

The Court DENIES the remaining requests to strike. Regarding Paragraph 8, Defendant's Exhibit A says nothing about whether the lease is commercial, and Plaintiff plainly alleges that she resided in the unit. Regarding Paragraph 16, the allegation that Defendant made inconsistent legal positions need not be stated with particularity and is relevant to Plaintiff's theories of retaliatory eviction and Defendant's bad faith in seeking to recover the property. Regarding Paragraphs 32 and 33, zoning regulations show that certain commercial uses are unpermitted and the Court must accept the truth of allegations that Plaintiff resided in the property. The request to strike Paragraph 50 is moot given the Court sustained a demurrer to the underlying cause of action with leave to amend. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances: Michael Vroman of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP (415.278.9400) for defendant and moving party; Amanda Silk (559.916.0736) plaintiff and opposing party appearing in propria persona.

Discussed. The court modifies its ruling as follows: SUSTAINED with leave to amend all causes of action. Cause of action 3 improperly combines two causes of action. Cause of action 4 is uncertain as to when and to whom Plaintiff's complaints were communicated, and fails to allege other elements of Section 1942.5(a). Causes of action 5 and 8 are uncertain for the same reason as cause of action 4. Cause of action 5 also fails to elaborate as to how "misclassifying the unit" would violate the rent ordinance. Cause of action 8 does not allege sufficient facts to state the element of IIED that the conduct was extreme and outrageous. Cause of action 9 fails to allege elements of an interference claim and contradicts itself as to whether Plaintiff's business was ever "operated" because the Complaint also says the business was "unable to launch." Cause of action 10 does not allege elements of a UCL violation. Cause of action 11 fails to state a cause of action because it does not allege that Defendant's conduct exceeded this Court's order. On agreement of the parties, the court grants plaintiff's request for 30 days leave to amend. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
